address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: The second of two volumes containing the Báb's Commentary on the (divine) Names, covering (incompletely) Váḥids viii-xix (cf. F. 17 (BBF. 10), the companion first volume, covering Váḥids ii-vii). Like the Báb's other later works it is arranged in Váḥids of nineteen chapters, each devoted to one "Name" of God considered under four aspects. Several Váḥids and many chapters are missing from this copy.

msItem.note: Second volume of the work only.
msItem.title: Tafsīr al-asmāʼ
تفسير الاسماء
Commentary on the Names (vol. ii)
msItem.note: Colophon (f. 261a) dates completion of this copy to Monday, Sha'bán 17, AH 1302 (31 May 1885); Browne notes the accompanying Bábí calendar date is difficult to reconcile precisely with Bahá'í usage. A following Persian note states the exemplar from which this copy was made was itself imperfect, and that no collation was attempted. The scribe's name is not given, only "cyphers" which Browne could not get Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel to explain.

Váḥids viii-xi are described as "tolerably complete"; Váḥids xii, xiii, xiv, xvii, and xviii are wholly missing (blank space left for them); Váḥid xv is represented by only two chapters, Váḥid xvi by one. Browne supplies (f. 262a) the scribe's own index of the "Names" forming the titles of the missing chapters, given here only in summary rather than reproducing the full index of some 200 Names.

Ff. 262b-268b contain a long invocatory prayer stringing together all 361 "Names" of the complete work (19 Váḥids of 19 - the Bábí "Number of All Things" and the number of days in the Bábí year), of which Browne gives only the beginning and end.

Browne further tabulates marginal annotations (in red) found against a number of chapters, specifying the occasion or addressee of the revelation - among them, notes naming Mírzá Aḥmad Kátib, Mullá 'Abdu'l-Karím of Qazvín, and Mullá Ḥasan of Marágha.
msItem.incipit: الباب الاول من الواحد الثامن من الشهر الثامن من السنة فی معرفة اسم المرشد... بسم الله الارشد الارشد... (f. 2b)

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBF. 9 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts (Continuation)", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Oct., 1892): 637-710.

supportDesc.extent:
264 folios (numerous leaves blank throughout), plus two additional leaves (α-β, a pasted-in covering letter)
20.75 18

handDesc.handNote: Written in a clear and compact naskh; chapter headings and marginal annotations in red.

history.provenance: Brought from Persia to Cyprus under the circumstances detailed at F. 8 (BBF. 6); came into Browne's hands at the same time and under the same circumstances as F. 10 (BBF. 8) - the covering letter, dated Ramaḍán 5, AH 1308 (14 April 1891), is pasted into this manuscript after the last leaf. Forwarded by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel from Cyprus, received 7 May 1891.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. ", abbreviated by the OCIMCO project team; provenance expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábí Manuscripts (Continued)," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Oct. 1892, pp. 648-656.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBF_9.xml) into this Fihrist record: added the missing altIdentifier (BBF. 9), and rewrote summary, item notes, incipit sample, physical description, hand note, and provenance history from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 648-656). Arabic quotation is unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, Cyprus). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
